title: Configure Environment-Specific Lambda URLs for Metrics Glossary Services
description:
Configure the DataTools Pro Laravel application to use environment-specific URLs for the Metrics Glossary Lambda services (Service 1 and Service 2). Previously, all environments pointed to a single Lambda URL. The change ensures each environment (dev, stage, prod) calls its own corresponding Lambda endpoint.

## Service 1 (parsedashboard)
- Dev: API Gateway endpoint with `/dev/parsedashboard` stage
- Stage: API Gateway endpoint with `/stage/parsedashboard` stage
- Prod: API Gateway endpoint with `/prod/parsedashboard` stage

## Service 2 (Metrics Glossary)
- Dev, Stage, Prod: Each environment has a separate Lambda function URL
acceptance_criteria: Dev environment calls the dev Lambda endpoints for both services
Stage environment calls the stage Lambda endpoints for both services
Prod environment calls the prod Lambda endpoints for both services
Service URLs are configured via .env files, not hardcoded
Code is promoted across environments without URL changes needed
middleware_changes: Lambda service endpoint URLs moved from hardcoded values to environment-specific .env configuration. Each environment's .env file contains the correct service 1 and service 2 URLs.
implementation_notes: Initially encountered a Lambda ARN validation error when switching to the new API Gateway stage URLs. Resolved with help from Tom and Anton via Slack. Final implementation stores service 1 and service 2 endpoint URLs in each environment's .env file.
comment_summary: Ryan requested starting with dev and stage only. Waqar encountered a Lambda ARN validation error with the new endpoint and was directed to Slack for troubleshooting with Tom and Anton. Eventually resolved; all environments now have their own endpoint URLs configured in .env files.
